MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DAVID ZUCKERMAN, ZUCKERMAN & MCQUILLER, LLP Motion To Enforce Deposition Subpoena Issued To Barbara Gault For Production Of Business Records And Appearance At Deposition, Etc.

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS DAVID ZUCKERMAN and ZUCKERMAN & MCQUILLER, LLP'S Motion To Enforce Deposition Subpoena Issued To Barbara Gault For Production Of Business Records And Appearance At Deposition.

Off calendar per the moving party.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend First Amended Complaint; **Court Reporter Fees Paid**
Off Calendar Dec-29-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Rebecca Coll, Esq. (415.609.3275; rcoll@quadracoll.com) of Quadra & Coll LLP for Plaintiff.
Jennifer Saunders, Esq. (213.542.8000; jsaunders@hbblaw.com) of Haight Brown & Bonesteel LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FRANCES PETROCELLI'S Motion To Amend First Amended Complaint:

Plaintiff's motion to amend to file a Second Amended Complaint to add a sentence regarding Dr. Wilson's execution of the grant deed is denied as unnecessary. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 452, the Court liberally construes the amended complaint. Paragraph 20 of the amended complaint alleges that defendants "breached their duty to Petrocelli to use reasonable skill and care in advising Petrocelli regarding her estate planning and documenting her estate plan, including but not limited to ensuring that Wilson's execution of the 2011 Restatement, 2016 Restatement and 2016 Amendment [to the Trust Agreement] could not be subject to a challenge on the grounds of incompetence and undue influence." (emphasis added.) Paragraph 10 of the amended complaint expressly references defendants' alleged negligence with regard to the grant deed. Given these allegations, the Court concludes that it is unnecessary for plaintiff to file the Second Amended Complaint. Paragraph 18 of the amended complaint incorporates the charge of malpractice with respect to the grant deed into the causes of action. Defendants' claim that they will be prejudiced because of the need to conduct any additional discovery is unpersuasive. If, as plaintiff alleges, Defendants negligently failed to ensure that Dr. Wilson was competent in 2011 and 2016, it follows inescapably that the same issues as to his capacity would apply to his execution of the grant deed in 2013. The grant deed theory of liability is not a new theory that prejudices defendant. Indeed, defendant's motion for summary judgment directly addresses the enforceability of the grant deed and that issue has been a focus of this litigation. (See Defendant's MSJ P&A, 10:27-11:6, 13-15:8.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964. =(302/EPS)
